If a registered party has not provided all the documents that it is required to provide under sections 424, 424.1 and 429, the Chief Electoral Officer shall postpone providing the certificate for any quarter until the party has provided those documents.

The Receiver General shall, on receipt of a certificate, pay to the registered party out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund the amount set out in the certificate. The payment may also be made in whole or in part to any provincial division of the party, as authorized in writing by the leader of the party.

In this Act, provincial division means a division of a registered party for a province or territory in relation to which the leader of the party has provided the following to the Chief Electoral Officer:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the division and of the province or territory;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the party;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the address of the office at which records of that division are maintained and to which communications may be addressed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the names and addresses of the chief executive officer and other officers of the division;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of any registered agent appointed by the division; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration signed by the leader of the party certifying that the division is a division of the party.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: This Act applies to information provided under this subsection as if it were information referred to in paragraphs 366(2)(a) to (h).

Subsection.Text: Within 15 days after a change in the information referred to in subsection (4), the chief executive officer of the provincial division shall report the change in writing to the chief agent of the registered party.

Heading.Label: Division 3.1
Heading.TitleText: Registration and Financial Administration of Leadership Contestants

Section.Text: In this Division, personal expenses of a leadership contestant means his or her expenses that are reasonably incurred in relation to his or her leadership campaign and include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: travel and living expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: childcare expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: expenses relating to the provision of care for a person with a physical or mental incapacity for whom the contestant normally provides such care; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contestant who has a disability, additional personal expenses that are related to the disability.

Subsection.Text: If a registered party proposes to hold a leadership contest, the chief agent of the party shall file with the Chief Electoral Officer a statement setting out the dates on which the leadership contest is to begin and end.

A registered party that proposes to vary the leadership contest period or to cancel a leadership contest shall file with the Chief Electoral Officer a statement setting out, as the case may be, the amended beginning date or ending date or the fact of its cancellation.
The Chief Electoral Officer shall, in the manner that he or she considers appropriate, publish a notice containing the information referred to in subsections (1) and (2).

Every person who accepts contributions for, or incurs leadership campaign expenses in relation to, his or her campaign for the leadership of a registered party shall register as a leadership contestant.
For the purposes of this Part, a leadership contestant is deemed to have been a leadership contestant from the time he or she accepts a contribution or incurs a leadership campaign expense.
An application for registration as a leadership contestant must include the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the leadership contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the address of the place at which the records of the leadership contestant are maintained and to which communications may be addressed;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of the leadership contestant’s financial agent; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of the leadership contestant’s appointed auditor.

Subsection.Text: The application must be accompanied by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the signed consent of the financial agent to so act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the signed consent of the auditor to so act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration signed by the chief agent of the registered party holding the leadership contest certifying that the party accepts the applicant as a leadership contestant; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a statement containing the information referred to in paragraphs 435.3(2)(d) and (e) with respect to contributions received before the application for registration.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall register a leadership contestant who meets the requirements set out in subsections (1) and (2). In the case of a refusal to register, the Chief Electoral Officer shall indicate which of those requirements have not been met.

Section.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall maintain a registry of leadership contestants that contains the information referred to in subsection 435.06(1).

Subsection.Text: A leadership contestant may, subject to any terms and conditions that the contestant specifies, appoint leadership campaign agents authorized to accept contributions and to incur and pay leadership campaign expenses for the contestant.

Within 30 days after the appointment of a leadership campaign agent, the leadership contestant shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with a written report, certified by the contestant’s financial agent, that includes the name and address of the leadership campaign agent and any terms and conditions to which the appointment is subject. The Chief Electoral Officer shall enter that information in the registry of leadership contestants.

Section.Text: The following persons are not eligible to be the financial agent or a leadership campaign agent of a leadership contestant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an election officer or a member of the staff of a returning officer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a leadership contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an auditor appointed as required by this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is not an elector;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an undischarged bankrupt; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person who does not have the capacity to enter into contracts in the province in which the person ordinarily resides.

Subsection.Text: Only the following are eligible to be an auditor for a leadership contestant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is a member in good standing of a corporation, an association or an institute of professional accountants; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership of which every partner is a member in good standing of a corporation, an association or an institute of professional accountants.

Subsection.Text: The following persons are not eligible to be an auditor for a leadership contestant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: election officers and members of the staff of returning officers;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: chief agents of registered parties or eligible parties and registered agents of registered parties;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: candidates and official agents of candidates;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: electoral district agents of registered associations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: leadership contestants and their leadership campaign agents;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: nomination contestants and their financial agents; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: financial agents of registered third parties.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Where member of partnership appointed as agent
Subsection.Text: A person may be appointed as agent for a leadership contestant notwithstanding that the person is a member of a partnership that has been appointed as an auditor, in accordance with thr Act for the registered party.

Section.Text: A leadership contestant shall obtain from the financial agent or auditor, on appointment, their signed consent to so act.

Section.MarginalNote: Replacement of financial agent or auditor
Section.Text: In the event of the death, incapacity, resignation or revocation of the appointment of the financial agent or auditor, a leadership contestant shall without delay appoint a replacement.

Section.MarginalNote: Only one financial agent and auditor
Section.Text: A leadership contestant shall have no more than one financial agent and one auditor at a time.

Subsection.Text: No person who is not eligible to be the financial agent or a leadership campaign agent of a leadership contestant shall so act.
No person who is not eligible to be an auditor of a leadership contestant shall so act.
Within 30 days after a change in the information referred to in subsection 435.06(1), a leadership contestant shall report the change in writing to the Chief Electoral Officer.
A report under subsection (1) that involves the replacement of the auditor or financial agent of the leadership contestant must include a copy of the signed consent obtained under section 435.11.
The Chief Electoral Officer shall enter any change in the information referred to in this section in the registry of leadership contestants.

Section.Text: A leadership contestant who withdraws from the leadership contest shall file with the Chief Electoral Officer a statement in writing to that effect signed by the contestant and indicating the date of the withdrawal. The Chief Electoral Officer shall indicate the withdrawal in the registry of leadership contestants.

A registered party that withdraws its acceptance of a leadership contestant shall file with the Chief Electoral Officer a statement in writing to that effect signed by the chief agent of the party and indicating the date of the withdrawal. The Chief Electoral Officer shall enter the withdrawal of acceptance in the registry of leadership contestants.

A leadership contestant who withdraws in accordance with section 435.16 or whose acceptance is withdrawn in accordance with section 435.17 is relieved of the obligation to provide returns under section 435.31 for any period after the withdrawal.
The Chief Electoral Officer shall, on becoming aware that a leadership contestant of a registered party has failed to comply with any requirement under this Division, notify the party accordingly.

Heading.TitleText: Powers, Duties and Functions of Financial Agent

Section.Text: The financial agent of a leadership contestant is responsible for administering the contestant’s financial transactions for his or her leadership campaign and for reporting on those transactions in accordance with the provisions of this Act.

Subsection.Text: The financial agent of a leadership contestant shall open, for the sole purpose of the contestant’s leadership campaign, a separate bank account in a Canadian financial institution as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act, or in an authorized foreign bank as defined in that section, that is not subject to the restrictions and requirements referred to in subsection 524(2) of that Act.
The account must name the account holder as follows: “(name of financial agent), financial agent”.

All financial transactions of the leadership contestant in relation to the contestant’s leadership campaign that involve the payment or receipt of money are to be paid from or deposited to the account.
The financial agent of a leadership contestant shall close the account after the end of the leadership contest or the withdrawal or death of the contestant and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: after the subsequent disposal of any surplus leadership campaign funds in accordance with this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if there are unpaid claims at the end of the leadership contest, after those claims have been dealt with in accordance with this Act.

Subsection.Text: The financial agent shall, on closing the account, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the final statement of the account.
No person, other than a leadership campaign agent of a leadership contestant, shall accept contributions to the contestant’s leadership campaign.

No leadership campaign agent of a leadership contestant shall accept a transfer of funds from a registered party or registered association, except the transfer by a registered party of an amount out of a directed contribution as defined in subsection 404.3(2).
No person or entity, other than a leadership campaign agent of the leadership contestant, shall pay leadership campaign expenses, other than personal expenses, of the contestant.
No person or entity, other than a leadership contestant or one of his or her leadership campaign agents, shall incur leadership campaign expenses of the contestant.
No person, other than a leadership contestant or his or her financial agent, shall pay the contestant’s personal expenses.
A person who has a claim to be paid for an expense in relation to a leadership campaign shall send the invoice or other document evidencing the claim to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the leadership contestant’s financial agent; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the leadership contestant, if there is no financial agent.

Subsection.Text: A claimant is barred from recovery of a claim to be paid if the invoice or other document evidencing the claim is sent more than three months after the end of the leadership contest.

If a claimant dies before the end of the three-month period, a new three-month period begins, for the purposes of subsection (1), on the day on which the claimant’s legal representative becomes entitled to act for the claimant.
A claim for leadership campaign expenses that has been sent in accordance with section 435.23 must be paid within 18 months after the end of the leadership contest.
The requirement to pay a claim within 18 months does not apply to a claim in respect of which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the documents may be sent within a new period under subsection 435.23(3);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Electoral Officer has authorized payment under section 435.26;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a judge has authorized payment under section 435.27; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: proceedings have been commenced under section 435.28.

Section.Text: A contract involving a leadership campaign expense in relation to a leadership contestant is not enforceable against the contestant unless entered into by the contestant personally or by one of the contestant’s leadership campaign agents.

Section.MarginalNote: Irregular claims or payments — Chief Electoral Officer

Subsection.Text: On the written application of a leadership contestant, of the contestant’s financial agent or of a person with a claim to be paid for a leadership campaign expense in relation to a leadership contestant, the Chief Electoral Officer may, on being satisfied that there are reasonable grounds for so doing, in writing authorize the payment, through the contestant’s financial agent, of the amount claimed if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the invoice or other document evidencing the claim was not sent in accordance with section 435.23; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the payment was not made in accordance with subsection 435.24(1).

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may impose any term or condition that he or she considers appropriate on a payment authorized under subsection (1).

Section.Text: On the application of a person with a claim to be paid for a leadership campaign expense in relation to a leadership contestant or on the application of the contestant’s financial agent or the contestant, as the case may be, a judge who is competent to conduct a recount may, on being satisfied that there are reasonable grounds for so doing, by order authorize the payment, through the contestant’s financial agent, of the amount claimed if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the applicant establishes that an authorization under subsection 435.26(1) has been refused and that the invoice or other document evidencing the claim was not sent in accordance with section 435.23 or the payment has not been made in the four-month period referred to in subsection 435.24(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount claimed has not been paid in accordance with an authorization obtained under subsection 435.26(1) and the applicant establishes their inability to comply with the authorization for reasons beyond their control.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The applicant shall notify the Chief Electoral Officer that the application has been made.

Subsection.Text: A person who has sent a claim in accordance with section 435.23 may commence proceedings in a court of competent jurisdiction to recover any unpaid amount

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time, if the leadership contestant or his or her financial agent refuses to pay that amount or disputes that it is payable; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: after the end of the period referred to in subsection 435.24(1) or any extension of that period authorized by subsection 435.26(1) or section 435.27, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: An amount paid by the financial agent of a leadership contestant as a result of proceedings referred to in subsection (1) is deemed to have been paid in accordance with this Act.

An unpaid claim mentioned in a return referred to in subsection 435.3(1) that, on the day that is 18 months after the end of the leadership contest, remains unpaid, in whole or in part, is deemed to be a contribution of the unpaid amount to the leadership contestant made as of the day on which the expense was incurred.
Subsection (1) does not apply to an unpaid claim that, on the day referred to in that subsection,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is the subject of a binding agreement to pay;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is the subject of a legal proceeding to secure its payment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is the subject of a dispute as to the amount the leadership contestant was liable to pay or the amount that remains unpaid; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: has been written off by the creditor as an uncollectable debt in accordance with the creditor’s normal accounting practices.

Subsection.Text: A leadership contestant or a financial agent who believes that any of paragraphs (2)(a) to (d) applies to a liability to pay an amount shall so notify the Chief Electoral Officer before the day referred to in subsection (1).

As soon as practicable after the day referred to in subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer shall, in any manner that he or she considers appropriate, publish the list of claims that are deemed under subsection (1) to be contributions.

Heading.TitleText: Return on Financing and Expenses in a Leadership Campaign

Subsection.Text: The financial agent of a leadership contestant shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the following in respect of a leadership campaign:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a leadership campaign return, substantially in the prescribed form, on the financing and leadership campaign expenses for the leadership campaign;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the auditor’s report on the return, if one is required under subsection 435.33(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration, in the prescribed form, made by the financial agent that the return is complete and accurate; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration in the prescribed form made by the leadership contestant that the return is complete and accurate.

Subsection.Text: The leadership campaign return shall include the following information in respect of the leadership contestant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of leadership campaign expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of disputed claims that are the subject of proceedings under section 435.28;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of unpaid claims that are, or may be, the subject of an application under section 435.26 or 435.27;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the total contributions received by the leadership contestant and the number of contributors;

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: disclosure of all financial loans for the purposes of the campaign, including interest rates, repayment schedules and the name of the lender;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of each contributor who made contributions of a total amount of more than $200 to the leadership contestant, that total amount, as well as the amount of each such contribution and the date on which it was received by the contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of each contributor who made a contribution that includes a directed contribution as defined in subsection 404.3(2) out of which an amount has been transferred by the party to the contestant, the amount of the contribution, the amount of the directed contribution, the amount transferred, as well as the dates of the receipt of the contribution and of the transfer;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the commercial value of goods or services provided and of funds transferred by the leadership contestant to a registered party or a registered association; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of contributions received but returned to the contributor or otherwise dealt with in accordance with this Act.

Subsection.Text: Together with the leadership campaign return, the financial agent of a leadership contestant shall provide to the Chief Electoral Officer documents evidencing expenses set out in the return, including bank statements, deposit slips, cancelled cheques and the contestant’s written statement concerning personal expenses referred to in subsection 435.36(1).

If the Chief Electoral Officer is of the opinion that the documents provided under subsection (3) are not sufficient, the Chief Electoral Officer may require the financial agent to provide by a specified date any additional documents that are necessary to comply with that subsection.
For the purpose of subsection (2), other than paragraph (2)(h), a contribution includes a loan.
The documents referred to in subsection (1) must be provided to the Chief Electoral Officer within six months after the end of the leadership contest.
A leadership contestant shall, within six months after the end of the leadership contest, send to his or her financial agent the declaration referred to in paragraph (1)(d).
If a leadership contestant dies without having sent the declaration within the period referred to in subsection (7)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: he or she is deemed to have sent the declaration in accordance with that subsection; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the financial agent is deemed to have sent the declaration to the Chief Electoral Officer in accordance with subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: The financial agent of a leadership contestant shall, for the period beginning on the first day of the leadership contest and ending on the day that is four weeks before the end of the leadership contest, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with a return that includes the information required under paragraphs 435.3(2)(d) to (h).

The financial agent of a leadership contestant shall, for each of the three weeks after the end of the period referred to in subsection (1), provide the Chief Electoral Officer with such a return weekly.
A return referred to in subsection (1) or (2) shall be provided no later than one week after the end of the period to which it relates.

Section.MarginalNote: When contributions forwarded to Receiver General
Section.Text: The financial agent of a leadership contestant shall, without delay, pay an amount of money equal to the value of a contribution that the contestant received to the Chief Electoral Officer, who shall forward it to the Receiver General, if the name of the contributor of a contribution of more than $25, or the name or the address of the contributor having made contributions of a total amount of more than $200, is not known.

Subsection.Text: As soon as practicable after the end of a leadership contest, the auditor of a leadership contestant who has accepted contributions of $5,000 or more in total or incurred leadership campaign expenses of $5,000 or more in total shall report to the contestant’s financial agent on the leadership campaign return and shall, in accordance with generally accepted auditing standards, make any examination that will enable the auditor to give an opinion in the report as to whether the return presents fairly the information contained in the financial records on which it is based.
The auditor’s report shall include any statement that the auditor considers necessary if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the return does not present fairly the information contained in the financial records on which it is based;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the auditor has not received from the leadership contestant or his or her financial agent all the information and explanation that the auditor required; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: based on the examination, it appears that proper accounting records have not been kept by the financial agent.

Subsection.Text: The auditor shall have access at any reasonable time to all documents of the leadership contestant and may require the contestant and his or her financial agent to provide any information or explanation that, in the auditor’s opinion, is necessary to enable the auditor to prepare the report.

A person referred to in subsection 435.1(2) who is a partner or an associate of an auditor of a leadership contestant or who is an employee of that auditor, or of the firm in which that auditor is a partner or associate, may not participate, other than in the manner referred to in subsection (3), in the preparation of the auditor’s report.

Section.MarginalNote: Extended period for leadership contestants outside Canada

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection 435.3(6), a leadership contestant who is outside Canada when the other documents referred to in subsection 435.3(1) are provided shall, within 14 days after returning to Canada, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the leadership contestant’s declaration referred to in paragraph 435.3(1)(d).

Subsection.MarginalNote: No extended period for financial agent
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to excuse a leadership contestant’s financial agent from complying with his or her obligations under this Act to prepare the return on the contestant’s leadership campaign expenses and make a declaration concerning it referred to in paragraph 435.3(1)(c).

After the period referred to in subsection 435.3(6), the leadership contestant’s financial agent shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with an updated version of any document referred to in subsection 435.3(1) that relates to a claim involving

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an extended period of recoverability under subsection 435.23(3) because of the death of a claimant;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an authorization to pay under section 435.26;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an order to pay under section 435.27; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a disputed claim under section 435.28.

Subsection.MarginalNote: When no update for audit required
Subsection.Text: If the matters dealt with in the updated versions of the documents have been subject to an audit under section 435.33, an updated version of the auditor’s report need not be provided.

The leadership contestant’s financial agent shall provide an updated version of a document referred to in subsection 435.3(1) within 30 days after making a payment that is dealt with in the updated version.
A leadership contestant shall, within five months after the end of the leadership contest, send to his or her financial agent a written statement in the prescribed form that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the amount of any personal expenses that he or she paid and details of those personal expenses, including documentation of their payment; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: declares that he or she did not pay for any personal expenses.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to a leadership contestant who dies without having sent the written statement referred to in that subsection before the end of the five-month period referred to in it.
The Chief Electoral Officer may correct a document referred to in subsection 435.3(1) or 435.35(1), if the correction does not materially affect its substance.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Corrections at request of Chief Electoral Officer
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may in writing request the leadership contestant or his or her financial agent to correct, within a specified period, a document referred to in subsection 435.3(1) or 435.35(1).

Section.MarginalNote: Extension or correction — Chief Electoral Officer

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer, on the written application of a leadership contestant or his or her financial agent, may authorize

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the extension of a period provided in subsection 435.3(4) or 435.35(3); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the correction, within a specified period, of a document referred to in subsection 435.3(1) or updated document referred to in subsection 435.35(1).

Subsection.Text: An application may be made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under paragraph (1)(a), within the period provided in subsection 435.3(4) or 435.35(3), as the case may be; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under paragraph (1)(b), as soon as the applicant becomes aware of the need for correction.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may not authorize an extension or correction unless he or she is satisfied by the evidence submitted by the applicant that the circumstances giving rise to the application arose by reason of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the illness of the applicant;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the absence, death, illness or misconduct of the financial agent or a predecessor;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the absence, death, illness or misconduct of a clerk or an officer of the financial agent, or a predecessor of one of them; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: inadvertence or an honest mistake of fact.

Subsection.Text: A leadership contestant or his or her financial agent may apply to a judge who is competent to conduct a recount for an order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: relieving the contestant or financial agent from complying with a request referred to in subsection 435.37(2); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing an extension referred to in paragraph 435.38(1)(a) or correction referred to in paragraph 435.38(1)(b).

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The applicant shall notify the Chief Electoral Officer of the application.

Subsection.Text: An application may be made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under paragraph (1)(a), within the specified period referred to in subsection 435.37(2) or within the two weeks after the expiration of that period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under paragraph (1)(b), within two weeks after, as the case may be,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the rejection of an application, made in accordance with section 435.38, for the extension or correction, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the expiration of the extended period or specified period authorized under paragraph 435.38(1)(a) or (b).

Subsection.Text: A judge may not grant an order unless he or she is satisfied that the circumstances giving rise to the application arose by reason of one of the factors referred to in subsection 435.38(3).
An order under subsection (1) may require that the applicant satisfy any condition that the judge considers necessary for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Refusal or failure of financial agent

Subsection.Text: A judge dealing with an application under section 435.39 or 435.41 who is satisfied that a leadership contestant or a financial agent has not provided the documents referred to in subsection 435.3(1) in accordance with this Act because of a refusal by, or a failure of, the financial agent or a predecessor of the financial agent shall, by order served personally, require the financial agent or that predecessor to appear before the judge.
The judge shall, unless the financial agent or predecessor on his or her appearance shows cause why an order should not be issued, order in writing that he or she

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: do anything that the judge considers appropriate in order to remedy the refusal or failure; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: be examined concerning any information that pertains to the refusal or failure.

Section.MarginalNote: Recourse of contestant for fault of financial agent
Section.Text: A leadership contestant may apply to a judge who is competent to conduct a recount for an order that relieves the contestant from any liability or consequence under this or any other Act of Parliament in relation to an act or omission of the contestant’s financial agent, if the contestant establishes that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it occurred without his or her knowledge or acquiescence; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: he or she exercised all due diligence to avoid its occurrence.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The applicant shall notify the Chief Electoral Officer that the application has been made.

Subsection.Text: A leadership contestant or his or her financial agent may apply to a judge who is competent to conduct a recount for an order relieving the financial agent from the obligation to provide a document referred to in subsection 435.3(1) or 435.35(1). The contestant or financial agent shall notify the Chief Electoral Officer that the application has been made.

The judge may not grant the order unless he or she is satisfied that the applicant cannot provide the documents because of their destruction by a superior force, including a flood, fire or other disaster.
For the purposes of this Act, the applicant is relieved from the obligation referred to in subsection (1) on the date of the order.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — false, misleading or incomplete returns
Section.Text: No leadership contestant and no financial agent of a leadership contestant shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with a document referred to in subsection 435.3(1) or 435.35(1) that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contestant or the financial agent, as the case may be, knows or ought reasonably to know contains a material statement that is false or misleading; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: does not substantially set out the information required by subsection 435.3(2) or required to be updated under subsection 435.35(1).

Section.Text: The surplus amount of leadership campaign funds that a leadership contestant receives for a leadership contest is the amount by which the sum of amounts referred to in subsection 404.3(3), contributions accepted by the leadership campaign agents on behalf of the contestant and any other amounts that were received by the contestant for his or her leadership campaign and that are not repayable is more than the sum of the contestant’s leadership campaign expenses paid under this Act and the transfers referred to in paragraph 404.2(3)(a).

Section.MarginalNote: Notice of assessment and estimate of surplus campaign funds

Subsection.Text: If the Chief Electoral Officer estimates that a leadership contestant has a surplus of leadership campaign funds, the Chief Electoral Officer shall issue a notice of the estimated amount of the surplus to the contestant’s financial agent.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disposition of surplus by financial agent
Subsection.Text: The financial agent of a leadership contestant who has a surplus of leadership campaign funds but has not received a notice of estimated surplus under subsection (1) shall dispose of that estimated surplus within 60 days after the provision of the contestant’s leadership campaign return.

Section.MarginalNote: Period for disposal of surplus funds

Subsection.Text: The financial agent of a leadership contestant shall dispose of a surplus of leadership campaign funds within 60 days after receiving the notice of estimated surplus.
Surplus leadership campaign funds must be transferred to the registered party that is holding the leadership contest or a registered association of that party.

The financial agent of a leadership contestant shall, within seven days after disposing of the contestant’s surplus leadership campaign funds, notify the Chief Electoral Officer in the prescribed form of the amount and date of the disposal and to whom the surplus was transferred.

As soon as practicable after the disposal of a leadership contestant’s surplus leadership campaign funds, the Chief Electoral Officer shall, in any manner that he or she considers appropriate, publish a notice referred to in subsection (1).
Subsections 437(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

An official agent of a candidate shall open, for the sole purpose of the candidate’s electoral campaign, a separate bank account in a Canadian financial institution as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act, or in an authorized foreign bank as defined in that section, that is not subject to the restrictions and requirements referred to in subsection 524(2) of that Act.
The account must name the account holder as follows: “(name of official agent), official agent”.
Subsection 437(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

After the election or the withdrawal or death of the candidate, the official agent of a candidate shall close the account once any unpaid claim or surplus of electoral funds has been dealt with in accordance with this Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 438(1) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 450(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An unpaid claim mentioned in a return referred to in subsection 451(1) that, on the day that is 18 months after polling day for the election to which the return relates, remains unpaid, in whole or in part, is deemed to be a contribution of the unpaid amount to the candidate made as of the day on which the expense was incurred.
Paragraph 451(1)(c) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 451(2)(c) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 451(2)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of contributions received from any of the following classes of contributor: individuals, corporations, trade unions and associations as defined in subsection 405.3(3);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 451(2)(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contributor that is an association as defined in subsection 405.3(3),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the name and address of the association, the amount of its contribution and the date on which it was received by the candidate, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the name and address of each individual whose money forms part of the contribution, the amount of money provided by that individual that is included in the contribution and the date on which it was provided to the association;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of each other contributor in a class listed in paragraph (f) who made contributions of a total amount of more than $200 to the candidate, that total amount, as well as the amount of each such contribution and the date on which it was received by the candidate;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 451(2)(i) and (j) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the commercial value of goods or services provided and of funds transferred by the candidate to a registered party, to a registered association or to himself or herself in his or her capacity as a nomination contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the commercial value of goods or services provided and of funds transferred to the candidate from a registered party, a registered association or a nomination contestant; and

Subsection.Text: Section 451 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Together with the electoral campaign return, the official agent of a candidate shall provide to the Chief Electoral Officer documents evidencing expenses set out in the return, including bank statements, deposit slips, cancelled cheques, any statements and declarations provided to the official agent by virtue of paragraph 405.3(2)(c) and subsection 405.3(4) and the candidate’s written statement concerning personal expenses referred to in subsection 456(1).

If the Chief Electoral Officer is of the opinion that the documents provided under subsection (2.1) are not sufficient, the Chief Electoral Officer may require the official agent to provide by a specified date any additional documents that are necessary to comply with that subsection.

Section.Text: Paragraph 452(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the contributor of a contribution of more than $25, the name or the address of the contributor having made contributions of a total amount of more than $200 or the name of the chief executive officer or president of a contributor referred to in paragraph 451(2)(h.1) is not known.

Section.Text: Subsection 453(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Auditor’s report on return on election expenses

Subsection.Text: As soon as practicable after an election, the auditor of a candidate shall report to the candidate’s official agent on the electoral campaign return referred to in paragraph 451(1)(a) and shall, in accordance with generally accepted auditing standards, make any examination that will enable the auditor to give an opinion in the report as to whether the return presents fairly the information contained in the financial records on which it is based.

Section.Text: The portion of section 461 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Recourse of candidate for fault of official agent
Section.Text: A candidate may apply to a judge who is competent to conduct a recount for an order that relieves the candidate from any liability or consequence under this or any other Act in relation to an act or omission of the candidate’s official agent, if the candidate establishes that

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 464(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of any candidate who received 10% or more of the number of valid votes cast; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 464(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

On receipt of the certificate, the Receiver General shall pay the amount set out in it out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the official agent of any candidate named in the certificate as partial reimbursement for the candidate’s election expenses and personal expenses. The payment may be made to the person designated by the official agent.
The portion of subsection 464(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An official agent of a candidate shall without delay return to the Receiver General any amount received by him or her under subsection (2) that is more than 60% of the total of
Paragraph 465(2)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 50% of the sum of the candidate’s paid election expenses and paid personal expenses, less the partial reimbursement made under section 464, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 465(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 50% of the election expenses limit provided for in section 440, less the partial reimbursement made under section 464.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 465(2)(a) and (b) of the Act, as amended by subsections (1) and (2), are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the sum of the candidate’s paid election expenses and paid personal expenses, less the partial reimbursement made under section 464, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the election expenses limit provided for in section 440, less the partial reimbursement made under section 464.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 465(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

On receipt of the certificate, the Receiver General shall pay the amount set out in it out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the official agent of the candidate. The payment may be made to the person designated by the official agent.
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on September 1, 2000.

Section.Text: Sections 466 and 467 of the Act are replaced by the following:

On receipt of the documents referred to in subsection 451(1) and, if it applies, subsection 455(1), including the auditor’s report, and a copy of the auditor’s invoice for that report in an amount of $250 or more, the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide the Receiver General with a certificate that sets out the amount of the expenses incurred for the audit, up to a maximum of the lesser of 3% of the candidate’s election expenses and $1,500.
On receipt of the certificate, the Receiver General shall pay the amount set out in it to the auditor out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 468(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each candidate, including one who has withdrawn under subsection 74(1), who the Chief Electoral Officer is satisfied has provided the documents under section 451 and returned any unused forms referred to in section 477, in accordance with subsection 478(2); and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 468(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

On receipt of the certificate, the Receiver General shall pay out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund the amount of each listed candidate’s nomination deposit to his or her official agent. The payment may be made to the person designated by the official agent.

Section.Text: Paragraph 469(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: he or she is deemed for the purpose of section 464 to receive 10% of the valid votes cast in the electoral district in which he or she was a candidate; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 470(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each candidate is deemed to have obtained 10% of the votes that would have been validly cast at that deemed election; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 470(1)(c) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: on receipt of a certificate referred to in section 464 or 465, the Receiver General shall pay out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the candidate’s official agent — or may alternatively pay to the person designated by the agent — the lesser of

Section.Text: Paragraphs 471(3)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any funds that the candidate transfers, during the election, to a registered party or a registered association;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount of a reimbursement referred to in paragraphs (2)(b) and (c) that the candidate transfers to that registered party; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any funds transferred by the candidate under paragraph 404.2(2)(d).

Section.Text: Paragraph 473(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a candidate who was endorsed by a registered party, to that party or to the registered association of that party in the candidate’s electoral district; or

Section.Text: Section 476 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No registered agent of a registered party, financial agent of a registered association or financial agent of a nomination contestant shall transfer funds to a candidate after polling day except to pay claims related to the candidate’s electoral campaign.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 478:

Heading.Label: Division 5
Heading.TitleText: Nomination Contest Report and Financial Administration of Nomination Contestants

Section.Text: The definitions in this section apply in this Division.

Definition.Text: personal expenses means the expenses that are reasonably incurred by or on behalf of a nomination contestant in relation to a nomination campaign and include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: travel and living expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: childcare expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: expenses relating to the provision of care for a person with a physical or mental incapacity for whom the contestant normally provides such care; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contestant who has a disability, additional personal expenses that are related to the disability.

Definition.Text: selection date means the date on which a nomination contest is decided.

Subsection.Text: When a nomination contest is held, the registered party, or the registered association if the contest was held by the registered association, shall, within 30 days after the selection date, file with the Chief Electoral Officer a report setting out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the electoral district, the registered association and the registered party that the nomination contest concerns;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the date on which the nomination contest began and the selection date;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of each nomination contestant as of the selection date and of his or her financial agent; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the person selected in the nomination contest.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall, in the manner that he or she considers appropriate, communicate to each nomination contestant the information related to that contestant that was reported under subsection (1).
The Chief Electoral Officer shall, in the manner that he or she considers appropriate, publish a notice containing the information referred to in subsection (1).

Section.Text: For the purposes of this Part, a nomination contestant is deemed to have been a nomination contestant from the time he or she accepts a contribution or incurs a nomination campaign expense.
No nomination contestant shall, in relation to his or her nomination campaign, accept contributions or incur nomination campaign expenses unless he or she has appointed a financial agent.

Subsection.Text: The following persons are not eligible to be the financial agent of a nomination contestant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an election officer or a member of the staff of a returning officer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a candidate or a nomination contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an auditor appointed as required by this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is not an elector;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an undischarged bankrupt; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person who does not have the capacity to enter into contracts in the province in which the person ordinarily resides.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Where member of partnership appointed as agent
Subsection.Text: A person may be appointed as agent for a nomination contestant notwithstanding that the person is a member of a partnership that has been appointed as an auditor, in accordance with the Act for the registered party.

Section.Text: A nomination contestant shall obtain from the financial agent, on appointment, their signed consent to so act.
In the event of the death, incapacity, resignation or revocation of the appointment of the financial agent, a nomination contestant shall without delay appoint a replacement.
A nomination contestant shall have no more than one financial agent at a time.
No person who is not eligible to be the financial agent of a nomination contestant shall so act.

Subsection.Text: Within 30 days after a change in the information referred to in paragraph 478.02(1)(c), a nomination contestant shall report the change in writing to the Chief Electoral Officer.
A report under subsection (1) that involves the replacement of the financial agent of the nomination contestant must include a copy of the signed consent under section 478.06.

Heading.TitleText: Powers, Duties and Functions of Financial Agent

Section.Text: The financial agent of a nomination contestant is responsible for administering the contestant’s financial transactions for his or her nomination campaign and for reporting on those transactions in accordance with the provisions of this Act.

Subsection.Text: The financial agent of a nomination contestant shall open, for the sole purpose of the contestant’s nomination campaign, a separate bank account in a Canadian financial institution as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act, or in an authorized foreign bank as defined in that section, that is not subject to the restrictions and requirements referred to in subsection 524(2) of that Act.
The account must name the account holder as follows: “(name of financial agent), financial agent”.

All financial transactions of the nomination contestant in relation to the contestant’s nomination campaign that involve the payment or receipt of money are to be paid from or deposited to the account.

After the selection date, the financial agent of a nomination contestant shall close the account once any unpaid claims or surplus nomination campaign funds have been dealt with in accordance with this Act.
The financial agent shall, on closing the account, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the final statement of the account.
No person, other than the financial agent of a nomination contestant, shall accept contributions to the contestant’s nomination campaign.
No financial agent of a nomination contestant shall accept a transfer from a registered party or registered association.
No person or entity, other than the financial agent of a nomination contestant, shall pay nomination campaign expenses, other than personal expenses, of the contestant.
No person or entity, other than a nomination contestant or his or her financial agent, shall incur nomination campaign expenses of the contestant.
No person, other than a nomination contestant or his or her financial agent, shall pay the contestant’s personal expenses.

Section.Text: The limit for nomination campaign expenses — other than personal expenses as defined in section 478.01 — that is allowed for a nomination contestant in an electoral district is the amount

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is 20% of the limit that was allowed under section 440 for a candidate’s election expenses in that electoral district during the immediately preceding general election, if the boundaries for the electoral district have not changed since then; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the Chief Electoral Officer determines, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: No nomination contestant or financial agent of a nomination contestant shall incur nomination campaign expenses — other than personal expenses as defined in section 478.01 — in an amount that is more than the limit allowed for that electoral district under section 478.14.
No person or entity shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: circumvent, or attempt to circumvent, the limit referred to in section 478.14; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: act in collusion with another person or entity for that purpose.

Subsection.Text: A person who has a claim to be paid for an expense in relation to a nomination campaign shall send the invoice or other document evidencing the claim to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nomination contestant’s financial agent; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the nomination contestant, if there is no financial agent.

Subsection.Text: A claimant is barred from recovery of a claim to be paid if the invoice or other document evidencing the claim is sent more than three months after the selection date.

If a claimant dies before the end of the three-month period, a new three-month period begins, for the purposes of subsection (1), on the day on which the claimant’s legal representative becomes entitled to act for the claimant.

A claim for nomination campaign expenses that has been sent in accordance with section 478.16 must be paid within four months after the selection date, or in the case referred to in subsection 478.23(7), the polling day.
The requirement to pay a claim within four months does not apply to a claim in respect of which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the documents may be sent within a new period under subsection 478.16(3);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Electoral Officer has authorized payment under section 478.19;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a judge has authorized payment under section 478.2; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: proceedings have been commenced under section 478.21.

Section.Text: A contract involving a nomination campaign expense in relation to a nomination contestant is not enforceable against the contestant unless entered into by the contestant personally or by the contestant’s financial agent.

Section.MarginalNote: Irregular claims or payments — Chief Electoral Officer

Subsection.Text: On the written application of a person with a claim to be paid for a nomination campaign expense in relation to a nomination contestant or of the contestant’s financial agent or the contestant in relation to such a claim, the Chief Electoral Officer may, on being satisfied that there are reasonable grounds for so doing, in writing authorize the payment, through the contestant’s financial agent, of the amount claimed if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the invoice or other document evidencing the claim was not sent in accordance with section 478.16; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the payment was not made in accordance with subsection 478.17(1).

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may impose any term or condition that he or she considers appropriate on a payment authorized under subsection (1).

Section.Text: On the application of a person with a claim to be paid for a nomination campaign expense in relation to a nomination contestant or on the application of the contestant’s financial agent or the contestant, as the case may be, a judge who is competent to conduct a recount may, on being satisfied that there are reasonable grounds for so doing, by order authorize the payment, through the contestant’s financial agent, of the amount claimed if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the applicant establishes that an authorization under subsection 478.19(1) has been refused and that the invoice or other document evidencing the claim was not sent in accordance with section 478.16 or the payment has not been made in the four-month period referred to in subsection 478.17(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount claimed has not been paid in accordance with an authorization obtained under subsection 478.19(1) and the applicant establishes their inability to comply with the authorization for reasons beyond their control.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The applicant shall notify the Chief Electoral Officer that the application has been made.

Subsection.Text: A person who has sent a claim in accordance with section 478.16 may commence proceedings in a court of competent jurisdiction to recover any unpaid amount

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time, if the nomination contestant or his or her financial agent refuses to pay that amount or disputes that it is payable; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: after the end of the period referred to in subsection 478.17(1) or any extension of that period authorized by subsection 478.19(1) or section 478.2, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: An amount paid by the financial agent of a nomination contestant as a result of proceedings referred to in subsection (1) is deemed to have been paid in accordance with this Act.

An unpaid claim mentioned in a return referred to in subsection 478.23(1) that remains unpaid, in whole or in part, on the day that is 18 months after the selection date — or in the case referred to in subsection 478.23(7), after the polling day — is deemed to be a contribution of the unpaid amount to the nomination contestant made as of the day on which the expense was incurred.
Subsection (1) does not apply to an unpaid claim that, on the day referred to in that subsection,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is the subject of a binding agreement to pay;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is the subject of a legal proceeding to secure its payment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is the subject of a dispute as to the amount the nomination contestant was liable to pay or the amount that remains unpaid; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: has been written off by the creditor as an uncollectable debt in accordance with the creditor’s normal accounting practices.

Subsection.Text: A nomination contestant or a financial agent who believes that any of paragraphs (2)(a) to (d) applies to a liability to pay an amount shall so notify the Chief Electoral Officer before the day referred to in subsection (1).

As soon as practicable after the day referred to in subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer shall, in any manner that he or she considers appropriate, publish the list of claims that are deemed under subsection (1) to be contributions.

Heading.TitleText: Return on Financing and Expenses in a Nomination Campaign

Subsection.Text: The financial agent of a nomination contestant who has accepted contributions of $1,000 or more in total or incurred nomination campaign expenses of $1,000 or more in total shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the following in respect of a nomination campaign:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a nomination campaign return, substantially in the prescribed form, on the financing and nomination campaign expenses for the nomination campaign;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the appointment of an auditor is required under subsection 478.25(1), the auditor’s report on the return made under section 478.28;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration, in the prescribed form, made by the financial agent that the return is complete and accurate; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration, in the prescribed form, made by the nomination contestant that the return is complete and accurate.

Subsection.Text: The nomination campaign return shall include the following in respect of the nomination contestant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of nomination campaign expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of disputed claims that are the subject of proceedings under section 478.21;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of unpaid claims that are, or may be, the subject of an application under section 478.19 or 478.2;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of contributions received from any of the following classes of contributor: individuals, corporations, trade unions and associations as defined in subsection 405.3(3);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the number of contributors in each class listed in paragraph (d);

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a contributor that is an association as defined in subsection 405.3(3),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the name and address of the association, the amount of its contribution and the date on which it was received by the nomination contestant, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the name and address of each individual whose money forms part of the contribution, the amount of money provided by that individual that is included in the contribution and the date on which it was provided to the association;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of each other contributor who made contributions of a total amount of more than $200 to the nomination contestant, that total amount, as well as the amount of each such contribution and the date on which it was received by the contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a numbered company that is a contributor referred to in paragraph (f), the name of the chief executive officer or president of that company;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the commercial value of goods or services provided and of funds transferred by the nomination contestant to a registered party, a registered association, or a candidate;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the commercial value of goods or services provided and of funds transferred to the nomination contestant from himself or herself in his or her capacity as a candidate; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the contributions received but returned to the contributor or otherwise dealt with in accordance with this Act.

Subsection.Text: Together with the nomination campaign return, the financial agent of a nomination contestant shall provide to the Chief Electoral Officer documents evidencing expenses set out in the return, including bank statements, deposit slips, cancelled cheques, any statements and declarations provided to the financial agent by virtue of paragraph 405.3(2)(c) and subsection 405.3(4) and the contestant’s written statement concerning personal expenses referred to in subsection 478.31(1).

If the Chief Electoral Officer is of the opinion that the documents provided under subsection (3) are not sufficient, the Chief Electoral Officer may require the financial agent to provide by a specified date any additional documents that are necessary to comply with that subsection.
For the purpose of subsection (2), other than paragraph (2)(i), a contribution includes a loan.
The documents referred to in subsection (1) must be provided to the Chief Electoral Officer within four months after the selection date.

Despite subsection (6), if the selection date of a nomination contest falls within an election period for that electoral district or the 30 days before it, a nomination contestant may submit the documents referred to in subsection (1) within the period for candidates referred to in subsection 451(4).
A nomination contestant shall, within four months after the selection date, send to his or her financial agent the declaration referred to in paragraph (1)(d).
If a nomination contestant dies without having sent the declaration within the period referred to in subsection (8),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: he or she is deemed to have sent the declaration in accordance with that subsection; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the financial agent is deemed to have sent the declaration to the Chief Electoral Officer in accordance with subsection (1).

Section.MarginalNote: When contributions forwarded to Receiver General
Section.Text: The financial agent of a nomination contestant shall, without delay, pay an amount of money equal to the value of a contribution received by the contestant to the Chief Electoral Officer who shall forward it to the Receiver General, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the financial agent cannot determine to which of the classes listed in paragraph 478.23(2)(d) the contributor belongs; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the contributor of a contribution of more than $25, the name or the address of the contributor having made contributions of a total amount of more than $200 or the name of the chief executive officer or president of a contributor referred to in paragraph 478.23(2)(g) is not known.

Subsection.Text: A nomination contestant who has accepted contributions of $10,000 or more in total or incurred nomination campaign expenses of $10,000 or more in total must appoint an auditor without delay.
The following are eligible to be an auditor for a nomination contestant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is a member in good standing of a corporation, an association or an institute of professional accountants; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership every partner of which is a member in good standing of a corporation, an association or an institute of professional accountants.

Subsection.Text: The following persons are not eligible to be an auditor for a nomination contestant:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: election officers and members of the staff of returning officers;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: chief agents of registered parties or eligible parties and registered agents of registered parties;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: candidates and official agents of candidates;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: electoral district agents of registered associations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: leadership contestants and their leadership campaign agents;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: nomination contestants and their financial agents; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: financial agents of registered third parties.

Subsection.Text: Every nomination contestant, without delay after an auditor is appointed, must provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the auditor’s name, address, telephone number and occupation and a signed declaration by the auditor accepting the appointment.

If a nomination contestant’s auditor is replaced, the contestant must, without delay, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the new auditor’s name, address, telephone number and occupation and a signed declaration accepting the appointment.

Section.Text: A nomination contestant shall have no more than one auditor at a time.
No person who is not eligible to be an auditor of a nomination contestant shall so act.

Subsection.Text: An auditor of a nomination contestant appointed in accordance with subsection 478.25(1) shall, as soon as practicable after the selection date, report to the nomination contestant’s financial agent on the nomination campaign return for that campaign and shall, in accordance with generally accepted auditing standards, make any examination that will enable the auditor to give an opinion in the report as to whether the return presents fairly the information contained in the financial records on which it is based.
The auditor’s report shall include any statement that the auditor considers necessary if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the return does not present fairly the information contained in the financial records on which it is based;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the auditor has not received from the nomination contestant or his or her financial agent all the information and explanation that the auditor required; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: based on the examination, it appears that proper accounting records have not been kept by the financial agent.

Subsection.Text: The auditor shall have access at any reasonable time to all documents of the nomination contestant, and may require the contestant and his or her financial agent to provide any information or explanation that, in the auditor’s opinion, is necessary to enable the auditor to prepare the report.

A person referred to in subsection 478.25(3) who is a partner or an associate of an auditor of a nomination contestant or who is an employee of that auditor, or of the firm in which that auditor is a partner or associate, may not participate, other than in the manner referred to in subsection (3), in the preparation of the auditor’s report.

Section.MarginalNote: Extended period for nomination contestants outside Canada

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection 478.23(6), a nomination contestant who is outside Canada when the other documents referred to in subsection 478.23(1) are provided shall, within 14 days after returning to Canada, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the nomination contestant’s declaration concerning his or her nomination campaign return referred to in paragraph 478.23(1)(d).

Subsection.MarginalNote: No extended period for financial agent
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to excuse a nomination contestant’s financial agent from complying with his or her obligations under this Act to prepare the return on the contestant’s nomination campaign expenses and make a declaration concerning it referred to in paragraph 478.23(1)(c).

After the period referred to in subsection 478.23(6) or (7), as the case may be, the nomination contestant’s financial agent shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with an updated version of any document referred to in subsection 478.23(1) that relates to a claim involving

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an extended period of recoverability under subsection 478.16(3) because of the death of a claimant;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an authorization to pay under section 478.19;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an order to pay under section 478.2; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a disputed claim under section 478.21.

Subsection.MarginalNote: When no update for audit required
Subsection.Text: If the matters dealt with in the updated versions of the documents have been subject to an audit under section 478.28, an updated version of the auditor’s report need not be provided.

The nomination contestant’s financial agent shall provide an updated version of a document referred to in subsection 478.23(1) within 30 days after making a payment that is dealt with in the updated version.
A nomination contestant shall, within three months after the selection date, send to his or her financial agent a written statement in the prescribed form that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the amount of any personal expenses that he or she paid and details of those personal expenses, including documentation of their payment; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: declares that he or she did not pay for any personal expenses.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to a nomination contestant who dies without having sent the written statement referred to in that subsection before the end of the three-month period referred to in it.
The Chief Electoral Officer may correct a document referred to in subsection 478.23(1) or 478.3(1), if the correction does not materially affect its substance.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Corrections at request of Chief Electoral Officer
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may in writing request the nomination contestant or his or her financial agent to correct, within a specified period, a document referred to in subsection 478.23(1) or 478.3(1).

Section.MarginalNote: Extension or correction — Chief Electoral Officer

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer, on the written application of a nomination contestant or his or her financial agent, may authorize

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the extension of a period provided in subsection 478.23(6) or 478.3(3); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the correction, within a specified period, of a document referred to in subsection 478.23(1) or updated document referred to in subsection 478.3(1).

Subsection.Text: An application may be made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under paragraph (1)(a), within the period provided in subsection 478.23(6) or 478.3(3), as the case may be; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under paragraph (1)(b), as soon as the applicant becomes aware of the need for correction.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may not authorize an extension or correction unless he or she is satisfied by the evidence submitted by the applicant that the circumstances giving rise to the application arose by reason of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the illness of the applicant;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the absence, death, illness or misconduct of the financial agent or a predecessor;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the absence, death, illness or misconduct of a clerk or an officer of the financial agent, or a predecessor of one of them; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: inadvertence or an honest mistake of fact.

Subsection.Text: A nomination contestant or his or her financial agent may apply to a judge who is competent to conduct a recount for an order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: relieving the contestant or financial agent from complying with a request referred to in subsection 478.32(2); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing an extension referred to in paragraph 478.33(1)(a) or correction referred to in paragraph 478.33(1)(b).

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The applicant shall notify the Chief Electoral Officer of the application.

Subsection.Text: An application may be made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: under paragraph (1)(a), within the specified period referred to in subsection 478.32(2) or within the two weeks after the expiration of that period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: under paragraph (1)(b), within two weeks after, as the case may be,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the rejection of an application, made in accordance with section 478.33, for the extension or correction, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the expiration of the extended period or specified period authorized under paragraph 478.33(1)(a) or (b).

Subsection.Text: A judge may not grant an order unless he or she is satisfied that the circumstances giving rise to the application arose by reason of one of the factors referred to in subsection 478.33(3).
An order under subsection (1) may require that the applicant satisfy any condition that the judge considers necessary for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Refusal or failure of financial agent

Subsection.Text: A judge dealing with an application under section 478.34 or 478.36 who is satisfied that a nomination contestant or a financial agent has not provided the documents referred to in subsection 478.23(1) in accordance with this Act because of a refusal by, or a failure of, the financial agent or a predecessor of the financial agent shall, by order served personally, require the financial agent or that predecessor to appear before the judge.
The judge shall, unless the financial agent or predecessor on his or her appearance shows cause why an order should not be issued, order in writing that the agent or predecessor

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: do anything that the judge considers appropriate in order to remedy the refusal or failure; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: be examined concerning any information that pertains to the refusal or failure.

Section.MarginalNote: Recourse of contestant for fault of financial agent
